name: Ryukyu Fireworks Festival
note: The earliest fireworks festival in Japan to welcome summer, featuring Okinawan music and beach views.
venue: Ginowan Seaside Park Ginowan Tropical Beach
access: Take a temporary shuttle bus from Naha Bus Terminal, or get off at the Convention Center bus stop and walk for 5 minutes.
rain: The event will proceed in light rain; it will be canceled in severe weather.

name: Lake Toya Long-Term Fireworks Festival (Daily)
note: Fireworks are launched for 20 minutes every night from April 28 to October 31 at 20:45. Recommended for summer travel.
venue: Lake Toya Onsen Lakeside
access: About 20 minutes by Donan Bus from JR Toya Station to Lake Toya Onsen, then a 5-minute walk from the last stop.
rain: Held in rainy weather (canceled in strong winds)

name: Yokohama Port Festival Fireworks
note: A flagship event in the Kanto region in June, featuring laser light shows and musical performances.
venue: Inland Park Front Sea Area
access: 5 minutes on foot from Minatomirai Station on the Minatomirai Line, or 15 minutes on foot from Sakuragicho Station on the JR Line.
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in case of severe weather.

name: Kyoto Art Fireworks
note: A 30-minute precision synchronized music fireworks display with a scale of 10,000 shots in June.
venue: JRA Kyoto Racecourse
access: Directly connected from Yodo Station on the Keihan Electric Railway Main Line, a 2-minute walk.
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in case of severe weather.

name: Adachi Fireworks
note: A large fireworks display that kicks off Tokyo's summer season, featuring 15,000 fireworks launched in one hour.
venue: Arakawa Riverbank (between Chiyoda Line Bridge and Nishiarai Bridge)
access: 15-minute walk from JR, Tokyo Metro, or Tobu Railway's Kitasenju Station, or a 15-minute walk from Tobu Railway's Kosuge Station and Gotanno Station.
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather.

name: Atami Fireworks Festival (Summer Edition)
note: Held multiple times throughout the year, featuring a natural sound system with powerful bass due to the basin and bay topography.
venue: Atami Bay (Atami Port Facilities to Atami Sun Beach)
access: 15-minute walk from JR Atami Station
rain: Held rain or shine (canceled in case of severe weather)

name: Sumida River Fireworks Festival
note: Last Saturday of July
venue: Venue 1: Below Sakurabashi to Above Kototoibashi, Venue 2: Below Komagata Bridge to Above Umayabashi
access: Venue 1: 15-minute walk from Tobu Railway and Tokyo Metro Asakusa Station, Venue 2: 5-minute walk from Toei Subway Oedo Line and Asakusa Line Kuramae Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather

name: Tenjin Matsuri Fireworks
note: Fixed date
venue: Kawasaki Park (next to the Mint Bureau) & Sakuranomiya Park
access: 5-minute walk from JR Tozai Line Osakajo Kitazume Station or 5-minute walk from Keihan Electric Railway & Osaka Metro Temmabashi Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather

name: Fukuroi Enshu Fireworks Festival
venue: Fukuroi City Haranayagawa Riverside Park
access: 20-minute walk from Aino Station or Fukuroi Station on the JR Tokaido Main Line
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in case of severe weather

name: Abekawa Fireworks Festival
venue: Abekawa Riverside (near Tamachi 3-chome)
access: Approximately 40 minutes on foot from JR Shizuoka Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather (no postponement)

name: Toyota Oiden Festival Fireworks
venue: Shirahama Park Area (Shirahama-cho, Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture)
access: 10-minute walk from Meitetsu Toyota City Station / 10-minute walk from Aichi Loop Railway Shin-Toyota Station
rain: Held rain or shine; canceled in case of severe weather (no postponement)

name: Gion Kashiwazaki Fireworks Festival
venue: Kashiwazaki City Central Coast and Minatomachi Seaside Park Area
access: 25-minute walk from JR Kashiwazaki Station
rain: Held in light rain; postponed in case of severe weather

name: Doshin-UHB Fireworks Festival
venue: Toyohira River Riverside (between Minami-Ohashi and Horohira Bridge)
access: Accessible from Toyosui-Susukino Station, Gakuenmae Station, and Nakano Island Station (within walking distance)
rain: Postponed to August 7th in case of inclement weather

name: Makuhari Beach Fireworks Festival
venue: Makuhari Seaside Park (Mihama Ward, Chiba City)
access: 15 to 20 minutes on foot from Kaihin Makuhari Station on the JR Keiyo Line
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather or strong winds (no postponement)

name: Edogawa Fireworks Festival
note: First Saturday in August
venue: Edogawa Riverbank (in front of Shinozaki Park)
access: 15-minute walk from Toei Subway Shinozaki Station / 25-minute walk from JR Koiwa Station and Keisei Edogawa Station
rain: Cancelled in case of bad weather

name: Itabashi Fireworks Festival
note: Kanto's longest 700-meter Niagara Falls fireworks
venue: Arakawa Riverbank (Itabashi Ward, Tokyo)
count: 約15,000発（戸田橋花火大会と合算）
access: 20-minute walk from Toei Mita Line Hasune Station or Nishidai Station / 20-minute walk from JR Saikyo Line Ukima-Funado Station
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in case of severe weather (no postponement)

name: Okazaki Castle Summer Festival Fireworks
venue: Otagawa and Yahagigawa Riverbanks (Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture)
access: 10-minute walk from Meitetsu Higashi-Okazaki Station / 10-minute walk from Meitetsu Okazaki Koen-mae Station / 10-minute walk from Aichi Loop Railway Naka-Okazaki Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather or river flooding (no postponement)

name: Hokuriku Great Fireworks Festival
venue: Tedori River Riverside (Kawakita Town, Nomi District, Ishikawa Prefecture)
access: About 15 minutes by shuttle bus or taxi from JR Nomi-Nakagawa Station

name: Ashikaga Fireworks Festival
venue: Watarase River Tanakabashi Riverside (Ashikaga City, Tochigi Prefecture)
access: 5-minute walk from JR Ashikaga Station South Exit / 10-minute walk from Tobu Isesaki Line Ashikagashi Station
rain: In case of inclement weather, it will be postponed to August 2nd

name: Koga Fireworks Festival
venue: Koga Golf Links (Watarase River Riverside)
access: 5-minute walk from Shin-Koga Station on the Tobu Nikko Line / 20-minute walk from Koga Station on the JR Tohoku Main Line
rain: Postponed to August 3 in case of inclement weather

name: Matsue Suigo Festival Lake Fireworks Festival
note: Held over two consecutive days
venue: East Shore of Lake Shinji (Sodeyama, Matsue City)
access: About a 15-minute walk from JR Matsue Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather (no postponement)

name: Nagaoka Festival Fireworks
note: Fixed date (super popular)
venue: Shinano River Riverside (Nagaoka City)
access: About a 30-minute walk from JR Nagaoka Station (shuttle buses available on the day)
rain: Held in light rain. Postponed in case of strong winds or flooding (8/2 to 8/16, 8/3 to 8/17)

name: Chikugo River Fireworks Festival
venue: Chikugo River Riverside (Kurume City, Kyomachi)
access: About a 10-minute walk from JR Kurume Station (Suitengu Exit)
rain: In case of rain, it will be postponed to the following day

name: Sendai Tanabata Fireworks Festival
note: Pre-festival on a fixed date
venue: Hirose River Area (near Nishi Park)
access: About a 3-minute walk from Omachi Nishi Park Station on the Tozai Subway Line / About a 30-minute walk from Sendai Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather

name: Lake Biwa Grand Fireworks Festival
venue: Otsu Port Area (Hama-Otsu, Otsu City)
access: Approximately 5 minutes on foot from Keihan Electric Railway Biwako Hama-Otsu Station / Approximately 15 minutes on foot from JR Otsu Station
rain: Cancelled in case of inclement weather (no postponement)

name: Aomori Fireworks Festival
venue: Aomori Port (Aoi Umi Park)
access: About a 10-minute walk from the East Exit of Aomori Station on the JR and Aoimori Railway
rain: In case of inclement weather, it will be postponed to the next day (8/8)

name: Nagara River Fireworks Festival
venue: Along the Nagara River (between Nagara Bridge and Kinkazan Bridge)
access: About 20 minutes by temporary bus from JR Gifu Station
rain: The event will proceed in light rain. It will be postponed to August 22 in case of severe weather or flooding.

name: Kachimai Fireworks Festival
note: Beginning of the Bon Festival holiday
venue: Tokachi River Special Venue (Obihiro City)
access: About a 20-minute walk from JR Obihiro Station (approximately 5 minutes by shuttle bus)
rain: In case of inclement weather, it will be postponed to the next day (8/14)

name: Kanmon Straits Fireworks Festival
note: Cross-prefecture event (in competition with Fukuoka)
venue: Kanmon Straits (Shimonoseki side: Misaki Town Pier, Arukaport, Kamon Wharf)
access: 5 minutes by bus from JR Shimonoseki Station, get off at 'Uminonakamichi-mae' and walk for 1 minute
rain: Held rain or shine; canceled in case of severe weather (no postponement)

name: Takamatsu Sanuki Festival Fireworks
venue: Around Sanport Takamatsu (Takamatsu City)
access: About a 5-minute walk from JR Takamatsu Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather

name: Fukuyama Summer Festival Ashida River Fireworks Festival
note: During the Bon Festival
venue: Upstream of Ashida River Bridge (Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture)
access: About 20 minutes by special bus from JR Fukuyama Station
rain: Postponed to August 17 in case of inclement weather

name: Akagawa Fireworks Festival
note: Voted one of the most moving fireworks displays by Japanese fireworks enthusiasts, featuring a horizontal launch width of 700 meters.
venue: Akagawa Riverbank (Haguro Bridge to Mikawa Bridge) (Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture)
access: Approximately a 30-minute walk from JR Tsuruoka Station (no temporary buses available)
rain: Cancelled only in cases of safety concerns such as typhoons or thunderstorms (held in light rain)

name: Lake Suwa Fireworks Festival
note: Fixed date (10,000 fireworks level)
venue: In front of Suwa Lakeside Park, Lake Suwa
access: About a 10-minute walk from JR Kamisuwa Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather (held in light rain)

name: Miyazu Lantern Floating Fireworks Festival
venue: Shimazaki Park and the surrounding area of Miyazu Bay (Miyazu City, Kyoto Prefecture)
access: About a 10-minute walk from Miyazu Station on the Kyoto Tango Railway
rain: Not announced

name: Kumano Grand Fireworks Festival
venue: Shichiri Mihama Coast (Kumano City, Mie Prefecture)
access: About a 5-minute walk from JR Kumano City Station
rain: Postponed to backup dates in case of inclement weather (8/21, 8/24, 8/27)

name: National Fireworks Competition (Omagari Fireworks)
note: Last Saturday of August
venue: Omagari, Daisen City, on the banks of the Omono River (Akita Prefecture)
access: About a 30-minute walk from JR Omagari Station
rain: Possible postponement in case of severe weather

name: Hokkaido Art Fireworks Festival
note: Autumn fireworks
venue: Moere Numa Park (Sapporo City, Hokkaido)
access: Take a bus from Toho Line's Kanjodori-Higashi Station or Shindo-Higashi Station to Moere Numa Park stop
rain: Held in rain; canceled in case of severe weather

name: Sakata Fireworks Festival
venue: Mogami River Riverside Park (Tsutsumicho, Sakata City, Yamagata Prefecture)
access: About 10 minutes by free shuttle bus from JR Sakata Station (or approximately 45 minutes on foot)
rain: Held rain or shine; canceled in case of severe weather; no postponement

name: Katakai Fireworks Festival
note: The world's largest single-shot fireworks display, held over two consecutive days.
venue: Asahara Shrine Back Area, Katakai Town Fireworks Launch Site (Ojiya City, Niigata Prefecture)
access: Shuttle bus or taxi from JR Joetsu Line Ojiya Station.
rain: The event will proceed in the rain (rain gear is recommended).

name: Himeji Port Marine Fireworks Festival
venue: Himeji Port (Shikama Area) near Shikama Rinkai Bridge
access: Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Shikama Station on the Sanyo Electric Railway
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather (official announcement by 11 AM on the day)

name: Naniwa Yodogawa Fireworks Festival
note: Moved to autumn in 2026
venue: Yodogawa Riverside (from the Shindōsū Bridge to the Route 2 section)
access: Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Hankyu Jūsō Station / Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Hanshin Himeshima Station
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in bad weather; no postponement

name: Yatsushiro National Fireworks Competition
venue: Yatsushiro City Kumagawa River Green Space (Upstream of Shin-Hagihara Bridge)
access: About a 10-minute walk from JR and Hisatsu Orange Railway Yatsushiro Station
rain: Held in light rain; postponed to November 7 in case of severe weather

name: Tamagawa Fireworks Festival
venue: Tamagawa Riverbank (between Futakotamagawa Bridge and the Third Keihin Road)
access: Approximately 20 to 30 minutes on foot from Tokyu Futakotamagawa Station (Setagaya) / Approximately 15 minutes on foot from Futakoshinchi Station (Kawasaki)
rain: Held in rainy weather (umbrellas prohibited, raincoats recommended) - canceled in severe weather

name: Tokyo Bay Fireworks Festival
venue: Harumi Wharf Offshore Viewing Area, Harumi Futou Park
access: About a 15-minute walk from Kachidoki Station on the Toei Oedo Line
rain: Cancelled in case of inclement weather; no postponement

name: Tsuchiura All-Japan Fireworks Competition
note: One of the three major fireworks competitions
venue: Sakura Riverbank, Tsuchiura City (near Gakuen Ohashi downstream)
access: About a 30-minute walk from the west exit of Tsuchiura Station on the JR Joban Line (paid shuttle bus available)
rain: In case of inclement weather, it will be postponed to the following Saturday (11/14)

name: Chichibu Night Festival Fireworks
note: Winter festival fireworks
venue: Yoyama Park (Launch Site) / Along National Route 140 (Viewing Area)
access: Approximately a 15-minute walk from Seibu Chichibu Station / Approximately a 5-minute walk from Chichibu Station
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in case of severe weather

name: SBI Fireworks in Osaka Sennan
note: Summer fireworks on the southern side of Osaka Bay, held at the seaside in Izumisano City.
venue: SENNAN LONG PARK
access: About a 10-minute walk from Tarui Station on the Nankai Main Line.
rain: Cancelled in case of inclement weather.

name: Kobe Port Minato Fireworks
note: A series of fireworks displays over multiple nights in autumn, held at Kobe Port Meriken Park.
venue: Meriken Park (fireworks launched from offshore barges)
access: About a 10-minute walk from JR Motomachi Station East Exit / About an 8-minute walk from Exit 2 of Minato-Motomachi Subway Station.
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in case of severe weather.

name: Ashiya Summer Festival Fireworks
note: Ashiya Nada Seaside Park, late July summer festival
venue: Shioashiya Beach (Ashiya City, Hyogo Prefecture)
access: 30-minute walk from Hanshin Electric Railway Ashiya Station
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in case of severe weather (no postponement)

name: Inagawa Fireworks Festival
note: Held along the Inagawa River, on the border of Osaka and Hyogo Prefectures, during the Bon Festival period.
venue: Inagawa River Riverside (Kawanishi City, Hyogo Prefecture - Ikeda City, Osaka Prefecture)
access: About a 10-minute walk from Hankyu Kawanishi Noseguchi Station or Hankyu Ikeda Station.
rain: Not announced
